unique-grilled-cheese.name: Unique Grilled Cheese
instructions: Combine water, vinegar, sugar, red pepper flakes, and garlic in a small saucepan over medium heat; cook and stir until sugar is dissolved and sauce is slightly reduced, about 5 minutes.
Heat a skillet over low heat.

Spread butter onto 1 side of each bread slice. Spread cream cheese on the opposite side of 1 of the bread slices. Layer Cheddar cheese and pickles on top of the cream cheese layer. Drizzle 1 to 2 tablespoons sauce on the other slice of bread on the side opposite the butter; place bread on top of pickle layer, butter-side on the outside, creating a sandwich.
Cook sandwich in the hot skillet until cheese is melted and bread is browned, about 5 minutes per side. Remove top bread piece, add tomatoes to middle, and close sandwich.

universal-marinade.name: Universal Marinade
instructions: Using a Microplane, finely grate lemongrass, chile (seeds and all!), garlic, and ginger into a medium bowl. (Alternatively, you can chop very finely, then mash to a paste using the side of your knife.) Add brown sugar, oil, fish sauce, and soy sauce and mix well.

upside-down-caramel-apple-muffins.name: Upside-down Caramel Apple Muffins
instructions: Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C). Grease a 12-cup muffin tin.

Combine apples, ½ cup brown sugar, ½ cup butter, and 1 pinch salt in a large skillet over medium-high heat; cook, stirring occasionally, until soft and syrupy, about 30 minutes. Stir in walnuts. Divide apple mixture among the prepared muffin cups. Set aside.
Whisk flour, ½ cup brown sugar, baking powder, apple pie spice, baking soda, and ⅛ teaspoon salt together in a large bowl until well combined; make a small well in the center. Set aside.

Whisk ¾ cup melted butter, applesauce, buttermilk, egg, and vanilla extract together in a medium bowl until smooth; pour into well in flour mixture. Mix until just combined; batter should be lumpy. Pour batter over apple mixture in the muffin cups.

Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into centers comes out clean, 15 to 20 minutes. Cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes. Run a knife around edges of each muffin, then carefully invert the tin onto a serving platter. Serve muffins hot.
